properties.name: The Albany Bulb
properties.hook: A former construction landfill that turned into an unsanctioned, open-air museum of junk sculpture and graffiti, jutting into the Bay with a full view of the Golden Gate. Nobody planned it. It just grew there.

properties.name: The Mission
properties.hook: The one San Francisco neighborhood where you should have no plan. Just walk. It's the warmest, densest, most argued-over square mile in the city, and it rewards getting lost more than anywhere else in the Bay.

properties.name: Tomales Point & the Tule Elk Reserve
properties.hook: A herd of elk on the narrowing tip of a peninsula, ocean on your left, bay on your right, and almost no one else out here. It's the closest thing the Bay Area has to a wildlife documentary you can walk into.
